title: Feature Request: Paste Commands
author: 1nv1n
body_snippet:
<!-- Feature Request -->
Use-Case:
I'm looking for a way to have some saved commands to be automatically be pasted (after a set delay) onto an open SSH session. Something similar to the "Macros" concept that exists on MobaXterm.

eg: In a session, I would have to type in "Command A", followed by "Command B" & so on. If there could be a feature that automate this, it would be greatly appreciated.

Related Reddit thread: https://www.reddit.com/r/mRemoteNG/comments/5wxom0/q_paste_commands/

iterations.description: Paste/macro command support is a frequently requested feature with community demand, enabling users to send predefined command sequences to SSH/terminal sessions

comments.author: asmerovski
comments.snippet: +1 for macro support! It is a killer feature, and I always go back to Mobaxterm just because of this feature. Please implement this, so I can finally ditch mobaxterm and its ugly UI.

comments.author: antoinekh
comments.snippet:
+1 for this one.
It's really handy when I need to type several commands on a regular basis

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet: Not sure, but this might require a different backend than Putty. #181 might be a solution for that, but it looks like it's a long way to get there...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

notes:
AI triage: Paste/macro command support is a frequently requested feature with community demand, enabling users to send predefined command sequences to SSH/terminal sessions
Approach: Add a 'Paste Commands' or macro system that allows users to define reusable command snippets and send them to active terminal sessions via SendKeys/SendMessage to the embedded PuTTY window, accessible from context menu and toolbar
